papers.title: When Do Hallucinations Arise? A Graph Perspective on the Evolution of Path Reuse and Path Compression
papers.abstract: Reasoning hallucinations in large language models (LLMs) often appear as fluent yet unsupported conclusions that violate either the given context or underlying factual knowledge. Although such failures are widely observed, the mechanisms by which decoder-only Transformers produce them remain poorly understood. We model next-token prediction as a graph search process over an underlying graph, where entities correspond to nodes and learned transitions form edges. From this perspective, contextual reasoning is a constrained search over a sampled subgraph (intrinsic reasoning), while context-free queries rely on memorized structures in the underlying graph (extrinsic reasoning). We show that reasoning hallucinations arise from two fundamental mechanisms: \textbf{Path Reuse}, where memorized knowledge overrides contextual constraints during early training, and \textbf{Path Compression}, where frequently traversed multi-step paths collapse into shortcut edges in later training. Together, these mechanisms provide a unified explanation for reasoning hallucinations in LLMs and connected to well-known behaviors observed in downstream applications.

analysis.tldr: 将LLM推理幻觉归因于路径复用和路径压缩两种图演化机制。
analysis.research_motivation: LLM推理幻觉普遍存在但机制不明，缺乏统一理论解释。
analysis.problem: 解释decoder-only Transformer产生推理幻觉的底层机制。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 推理幻觉源于两种机制：早期训练中记忆知识覆盖上下文约束（路径复用），后期训练中高频多步路径坍缩为捷径（路径压缩）。
analysis.method: 将next-token预测建模为图搜索过程，分析路径复用和路径压缩两种机制。
contributions: 提出图视角统一解释LLM推理幻觉的两种机制：路径复用和路径压缩。
将幻觉机制与下游应用中的已知行为联系起来。
analysis.experiments: 摘要未提供具体实验结果。
limitations: 可能缺乏大规模实证验证。
机制解释可能过于简化。
analysis.primary_area_en: foundation or frontier models, including LLMs
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 将LLM推理幻觉归因于路径复用和路径压缩两种图演化机制。

papers.title: Design Conditions for Intra-Group Learning of Sequence-Level Rewards: Token Gradient Cancellation
papers.abstract: In sparse termination rewards, intra-group comparisons have become the dominant paradigm for fine-tuning reasoning models via reinforcement learning. However, long-term training often leads to issues like ineffective update accumulation (learning tax), solution probability drift, and entropy collapse. This paper presents a necessary condition for algorithm design from a token-level credit assignment perspective: to prevent reward-irrelevant drift, intra-group objectives must maintain gradient exchangeability across token updates, enabling gradient cancellation on weak-credit/high-frequency tokens. We show that two common mechanisms disrupting exchangeability make "non-cancellation" a structural norm. Based on this, we propose minimal intra-group transformations to restore or approximate the cancellation structure in the shared token space. Experimental results demonstrate that these transformations stabilize training, improve sample efficiency, and enhance final performance, validating the value of this design condition.

analysis.tldr: 从token级信用分配角度提出组内学习序列奖励的设计条件——梯度可交换性，通过最小变换恢复梯度抵消结构以稳定训练。
analysis.research_motivation: 稀疏终止奖励下，组内比较成为微调推理模型的主流范式，但长期训练常导致更新积累无效、解概率漂移和熵坍塌等问题，亟需理论指导的算法设计条件。
analysis.problem: 组内学习序列奖励时，如何设计目标函数以避免奖励无关的漂移和训练不稳定。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要指出两种破坏梯度可交换性的常见机制使得“非抵消”成为结构常态，导致弱信用/高频token上的梯度无法抵消。
analysis.method: 从token级信用分配视角提出梯度可交换性作为必要设计条件，并设计最小组内变换来恢复或近似共享token空间中的抵消结构。
contributions: 揭示了组内学习序列奖励中梯度可交换性的必要性，并指出两种破坏机制导致非抵消成为结构常态。
提出最小组内变换以恢复或近似抵消结构，实验验证其能稳定训练、提升样本效率和最终性能。
analysis.experiments: 摘要未提供具体实验结果。
limitations: 可能仅适用于特定类型的序列奖励场景。
最小变换的具体实现可能依赖任务特性。
analysis.primary_area_en: reinforcement learning
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 从token级信用分配角度提出组内学习序列奖励的设计条件——梯度可交换性，通过最小变换恢复梯度抵消结构以稳定训练。

papers.title: Focus Matters: Phase-Aware Suppression for Hallucination in Vision-Language Models
papers.abstract: Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) have achieved impressive progress in multimodal reasoning, yet they remain prone to object hallucinations, generating descriptions of objects that are not present in the input image. Recent approaches attempt to mitigate hallucinations by suppressing unreliable visual signals in the vision encoder, but many rely on iterative optimization for each input, resulting in substantial inference latency. In this work, we investigate the internal attention dynamics of vision encoders in LVLMs and identify a consistent three-phase structure of visual information processing: diffusion, focus, and rediffusion. Our analysis reveals that hallucination behavior is particularly sensitive to tokens receiving low attention during the focus phase. Motivated by this observation, we propose a lightweight inference-time intervention that selectively suppresses such tokens during the focus phase. The method operates in a training-free manner using statistics from a single forward pass and employs a Determinantal Point Process (DPP) to preserve diverse visual cues while filtering redundant tokens. Extensive experiments across multiple LVLM backbones and decoding strategies demonstrate that the proposed approach consistently reduces hallucination metrics while maintaining competitive caption quality. Moreover, compared to adversarial uncertainty estimation methods, our approach achieves comparable hallucination mitigation with negligible additional inference latency.

analysis.tldr: 通过分析视觉编码器中的三阶段注意力动态，提出一种轻量级、无需训练的推理时干预方法，在焦点阶段抑制低注意力token以减少幻觉。
analysis.research_motivation: 大型视觉语言模型（LVLMs）在多模态推理中易产生对象幻觉，现有方法依赖迭代优化导致高推理延迟，亟需高效且有效的幻觉抑制方法。
analysis.problem: 如何在不增加推理延迟的前提下，有效抑制LVLMs中的对象幻觉。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 发现视觉编码器内部注意力动态存在一致的三个阶段：扩散、焦点和再扩散，且幻觉行为对焦点阶段中低注意力token特别敏感。
analysis.method: 提出一种轻量级推理时干预方法，基于单次前向传播的统计信息，在焦点阶段使用行列式点过程（DPP）选择性抑制低注意力token，同时保留多样视觉线索。
contributions: 揭示了LVLM视觉编码器中视觉信息处理的三阶段注意力动态结构，并发现幻觉与焦点阶段低注意力token的关联。
提出一种训练无关、单次前向传播的轻量级推理时干预方法，利用DPP抑制低注意力token，显著降低幻觉且几乎不增加延迟。
analysis.experiments: 在多个LVLM主干和多种解码策略上进行实验，结果表明该方法一致降低幻觉指标，同时保持有竞争力的描述质量；与对抗性不确定性估计方法相比，实现了相当的幻觉缓解且额外推理延迟可忽略。
limitations: 可能仅适用于具有类似三阶段注意力动态的视觉编码器。
DPP的多样性保持效果可能在某些场景下不足。
analysis.primary_area_en: applications to computer vision, audio, language, and other modalities
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 通过分析视觉编码器中的三阶段注意力动态，提出一种轻量级、无需训练的推理时干预方法，在焦点阶段抑制低注意力token以减少幻觉。

papers.title: HEDGE: Heterogeneous Ensemble for Detection of AI-GEnerated Images in the Wild
papers.abstract: Robust detection of AI-generated images in the wild remains challenging due to the rapid evolution of generative models and varied real-world distortions. We argue that relying on a single training regime, resolution, or backbone is insufficient to handle all conditions, and that structured heterogeneity across these dimensions is essential for robust detection. To this end, we propose HEDGE, a Heterogeneous Ensemble for Detection of AI-GEnerated images, that introduces complementary detection routes along three axes: diverse training data with strong augmentation, multi-scale feature extraction, and backbone heterogeneity. Specifically, Route~A progressively constructs DINOv3-based detectors through staged data expansion and augmentation escalation, Route~B incorporates a higher-resolution branch for fine-grained forensic cues, and Route~C adds a MetaCLIP2-based branch for backbone diversity. All outputs are fused via logit-space weighted averaging, refined by a lightweight dual-gating mechanism that handles branch-level outliers and majority-dominated fusion errors. HEDGE achieves 4th place in the NTIRE 2026 Robust AI-Generated Image Detection in the Wild Challenge and attains state-of-the-art performance with strong robustness on multiple AIGC image detection benchmarks.

analysis.tldr: HEDGE通过多维度异构集成（数据增强、多尺度特征、不同骨干网络）提升AI生成图像检测的鲁棒性。
analysis.research_motivation: 现有AI生成图像检测方法因生成模型快速演化和现实失真多样而鲁棒性不足，单一训练策略或骨干网络难以应对所有条件。
analysis.problem: 如何设计一个能适应多种生成模型和现实失真的鲁棒AI生成图像检测系统。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要指出单一训练策略、分辨率或骨干网络不足以处理所有条件，结构化异构性对鲁棒检测至关重要。
analysis.method: 提出HEDGE异构集成方法，包含三条互补检测路线：路线A通过渐进式数据扩展和增强构建DINOv3检测器；路线B使用更高分辨率分支提取细粒度取证线索；路线C引入MetaCLIP2分支增加骨干多样性。输出通过logit空间加权平均融合，并由轻量级双门控机制处理分支异常和多数主导融合错误。
contributions: 提出多维度异构集成框架HEDGE，结合数据增强、多尺度特征和骨干网络多样性。
引入轻量级双门控融合机制，提升集成鲁棒性。
在NTIRE 2026挑战赛中获得第4名，并在多个AIGC检测基准上达到SOTA。
analysis.experiments: 在NTIRE 2026 Robust AI-Generated Image Detection in the Wild Challenge中取得第4名，并在多个AIGC图像检测基准上达到SOTA，具有强鲁棒性。
limitations: 集成方法可能增加计算开销。
对未见过的生成模型或极端失真的泛化能力未充分验证。
analysis.primary_area_en: applications to computer vision, audio, language, and other modalities
analysis.one_sentence_summary: HEDGE通过多维度异构集成（数据增强、多尺度特征、不同骨干网络）提升AI生成图像检测的鲁棒性。

papers.title: Towards the AI Historian: Agentic Information Extraction from Primary Sources
papers.abstract: AI is supporting, accelerating, and automating scientific discovery across a diverse set of fields. However, AI adoption in historical research remains limited due to the lack of solutions designed for historians. In this technical progress report, we introduce the first module of Chronos, an AI Historian under development. This module enables historians to convert image scans of primary sources into data through natural-language interactions. Rather than imposing a fixed extraction pipeline powered by a vision-language model (VLM), it allows historians to adapt workflows for heterogeneous source corpora, evaluate the performance of AI models on specific tasks, and iteratively refine workflows through natural-language interaction with the Chronos agent. The module is open-source and ready to be used by historical researchers on their own sources.

analysis.tldr: 提出Chronos AI历史学家的首个模块，通过自然语言交互从历史原始资料图像中提取结构化数据。
analysis.research_motivation: 历史研究中AI应用有限，缺乏专为历史学家设计的解决方案，现有方法无法灵活适应异构原始资料集。
analysis.problem: 如何让历史学家通过自然语言交互，从原始资料图像中灵活、可评估地提取结构化数据。

contributions: 提出Chronos AI历史学家的首个模块，实现从原始资料图像到数据的自然语言交互式提取。
开源模块，支持历史学家在自有资料上使用，并允许灵活调整工作流和评估模型性能。

papers.title: CRAFT: Video Diffusion for Bimanual Robot Data Generation
papers.abstract: Bimanual robot learning from demonstrations is fundamentally limited by the cost and narrow visual diversity of real-world data, which constrains policy robustness across viewpoints, object configurations, and embodiments. We present Canny-guided Robot Data Generation using Video Diffusion Transformers (CRAFT), a video diffusion-based framework for scalable bimanual demonstration generation that synthesizes temporally coherent manipulation videos while producing action labels. By conditioning video diffusion on edge-based structural cues extracted from simulator-generated trajectories, CRAFT produces physically plausible trajectory variations and supports a unified augmentation pipeline spanning object pose changes, camera viewpoints, lighting and background variations, cross-embodiment transfer, and multi-view synthesis. We leverage a pre-trained video diffusion model to convert simulated videos, along with action labels from the simulation trajectories, into action-consistent demonstrations. Starting from only a few real-world demonstrations, CRAFT generates a large, visually diverse set of photorealistic training data, bypassing the need to replay demonstrations on the real robot (Sim2Real). Across simulated and real-world bimanual tasks, CRAFT improves success rates over existing augmentation strategies and straightforward data scaling, demonstrating that diffusion-based video generation can substantially expand demonstration diversity and improve generalization for dual-arm manipulation tasks. Our project website is available at: https://craftaug.github.io/

analysis.tldr: CRAFT 利用视频扩散模型从少量真实演示生成多样化双臂操作数据，提升策略泛化性。
analysis.research_motivation: 真实双臂操作数据采集成本高、视觉多样性有限，限制了策略的鲁棒性和泛化能力。
analysis.problem: 如何从少量真实演示中生成大规模、视觉多样且动作一致的双臂操作训练数据。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 基于视频扩散变换器，以模拟轨迹的边缘结构线索为条件，生成时序连贯的操作视频并附带动作标签，支持物体姿态、视角、光照、背景、跨本体和多视角合成等统一增强。
contributions: 提出 CRAFT 框架，利用视频扩散模型从少量真实演示生成大规模多样化双臂操作数据。
在模拟和真实双臂任务上，CRAFT 相比现有增强策略和数据缩放方法提升了成功率。
analysis.experiments: 在模拟和真实双臂任务上评估，CRAFT 提升了成功率。
limitations: 依赖预训练视频扩散模型，可能受限于模型能力。
